writable_scopes: any *.js, *.json, *.css path that Claude Code Write/Edit just touched, regardless of repo location
scope is NOT restricted by the project deny-list (those apply to Claude Code's Write/Edit, not to the bash subprocess the hook spawns)
in practice: every .js/.json/.css under the repo root + anywhere else the operator's npm/npx PATH resolves a biome binary AND the just-written path can be reached

principals.approval_policy: AUTO-APPROVED — fires unconditionally on every Write|Edit matcher hit with no operator prompt; 10-second timeout
principals.approval_precedence: post-tool — runs AFTER the Write/Edit completes; cannot prevent the write, only re-format the result
outbound_network_scope: npx may resolve+fetch packages on first run (network); after install, none
principals.audit_attention: AAF-VGH-006 — error suppression makes failures invisible: silenced stderr (2>/dev/null), unconditional exit 0, 10-second timeout. Failure modes: (1) npx not available, (2) biome missing/not installed, (3) biome parse error in file, (4) timeout, (5) jq missing, (6) malformed input JSON, (7) file write race. None are observable from logs, exit code, or surface telemetry — see Phase 7 §3.4 and Phase 7 flag no-runtime-action-provenance for the cross-phase tie.

aaf_resolution.verdict: confirmed — write-capable automation principal with auto-approved policy and no failure reporting surface
aaf_resolution.severity_in_phase_10: moderate — file corruption from a biome parse error is recoverable (git revert); the architectural concern is the precedent of a write-capable, auto-approved, silent automation
ff_candidates: FF-claude-code-hook-observability — every PostToolUse hook must (1) tee stderr to a known log path, (2) propagate biome's exit code, (3) emit a structured event (line per invocation) with timestamp + file_path + biome_exit_code
FF-claude-code-hook-scope-declaration — every hook must declare its filesystem-scope as part of the hook object (path-prefix allowlist enforced at hook-invocation time, not in the inline bash)

principals.sandbox_model: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest runner; OIDC restricts the token's audience to GitHub Pages
principals.escalation_explanation: Workflow-root permissions block grants pages:write + id-token:write to EVERY job in the file (deploy.yml:10-13). Jobs `quality` and `build` do not need these elevated permissions — they inherit them. While the deploy action is the only consumer, the elevation is workflow-wide. Per-job permissions override (a `permissions:` block on each job) would scope this tighter. The current configuration is the GitHub-recommended Pages template; recording the escalation explicitly because §9.7 requires it.

aaf_resolution.verdict: confirmed — deploy-pages@v4 is @major-tag-pinned, not @SHA-pinned; the single point of supply-chain risk for the elevated principal
aaf_resolution.severity_in_phase_10: moderate — actions/deploy-pages is org-controlled by actions/ (high trust by Pages users); but a compromise or breaking change in @v4's HEAD propagates to this deploy on the next run. Symmetrical concern for the other 4 actions, but deploy-pages has the elevated authority.
ff_candidates: FF-pin-github-actions-to-sha — every action reference in .github/workflows/*.yml must use @<40-hex-sha>; comment with version tag for humans (renovate/dependabot can update + re-pin)
FF-per-job-actions-permissions — quality + build jobs should declare permissions: contents:read only; deploy job declares pages:write + id-token:write at job-level, not workflow-level

AAF-VGH-006.verdict: RESOLVED at A-04 — PostToolUse hook is a write-capable automation principal with auto-approved approval policy, no enforced filesystem scope, and silenced error reporting (2>/dev/null + unconditional exit 0). The flag combination ambient-authority + write-without-approval-policy + approval-not-enforced is intentional: this represents the cross-phase pattern that recurs in Phase 7 (no-runtime-action-provenance) and feeds Phase 10 findings.
ff_candidates: FF-claude-code-hook-observability — every PostToolUse hook tees stderr to .claude/logs/posttooluse.log and surfaces non-zero exit
FF-claude-code-hook-scope-declaration — every hook declares explicit filesystem-scope; out-of-scope writes refuse rather than silently no-op

AAF-VGH-009.verdict: RESOLVED at A-11 — context7 MCP declared in CLAUDE.md:27-33 + AGENTS.md:60-61 without .mcp.json in repo. Authority is undeclared-scope + remote-agent-token-scope-unknown. Paired with sibling AAF-NDM-008.

FF-mcp-tool-schema-recorded — every callable MCP tool referenced has an in-repo schema citation

AAF-VGH-010.verdict: RESOLVED at A-10 + third_party_action_authority_inventory — 5 actions @major-tag-only (not @SHA). The single high-authority surface is actions/deploy-pages@v4 because it receives the OIDC token and publishes to https://jvjohnson.dev. The other 4 actions are moderate-risk supply chain surfaces with same pin pattern.

cross_repo_authority_comparison.summary: Three repos diverge sharply on Claude Code authority declaration:

rows.permissions_model: broad allow (Read/Edit/Write + uv/ruff/pytest/mypy/mise/git-subcommands/gh-pr-list etc.) + explicit ask (git push, gh pr create, gh repo *) + explicit deny (rm -rf, git push --force, git reset --hard)
rows.ambient_authority: low — non-Bash tools (Read/Edit/Write) are EXPLICITLY in allow

rows.permissions_model: narrow allow (9 Bash patterns only) + deny (env paths + build artifacts)
rows.ambient_authority: moderate — Read/Write/Edit/Grep/Glob/Task NOT in allow; rely on Claude Code default per-call prompting
rows.approval_precedence_clarity: moderate — deny is explicit; allow is Bash-only; non-Bash defaults are tool-side, not project-scoped

rows.permissions_model: n/a — NO .claude/settings.json exists; only .claude/settings.local.json (gitignored, content unknown)
rows.ambient_authority: high — entire authority surface is operator-local and invisible to audit

common_ff_candidate.name: FF-claude-code-settings-canonicalization-across-repos
common_ff_candidate.rationale: Three operator-owned repos exhibit three different Claude Code authority models: (1) explicit ask/allow/deny matrix (planning-summer-2026), (2) Bash-only allow + path deny (verlyn13.github.io), (3) no project-scoped settings at all (new-direction-2026). Common operator + common agent surface + 3 inconsistent authority declarations = drift surface. A canonical .claude/settings.json template (or shared base profile) committed to each repo would (a) close the ambient-authority gap in this repo, (b) eliminate the no-settings gap in new-direction-2026, (c) align the three models so cross-repo workflows have predictable authority.
common_ff_candidate.failure_condition: missing .claude/settings.json; OR settings.json that doesn't declare Read/Write/Edit/Task in allow OR ask; OR settings.json with deny list smaller than the canonical-minimum (env paths + build artifacts + force-write git)
common_ff_candidate.implementation_notes: Could live as a shared .claude/settings.shared.json + a copy script, or as a meta-inventory FF that scans every repo on disk for the canonical pattern (meta-inventory already has scan_local_repos.py). Cross-cite Phase 10 FF candidate for sibling repos.

exit_check.exit_check_notes: All 4 write-capable paths from Phase 3 (operator, Claude Code, PostToolUse hook, deploy job) carry recorded authority. The PostToolUse hook is flagged write-without-approval-policy as an explicit value (it has auto-approved policy but no enforced scope), not an absence. The deploy job is flagged escalation because workflow-root permissions are inherited by all jobs rather than scoped per-job; the elevation itself is intentional per GitHub Pages pattern. All 3 SKILLs flagged subagent-without-policy with rationale recorded. context7 MCP flagged on 3 facets (remote-agent-token-scope-unknown + undeclared-scope + ambient-authority). 5 third-party actions inventoried separately as supply-chain authority surfaces with @v*-not-@SHA flag.
